summary: PostgreSQL CI job fails because the new Alembic unified schema enforces constraints that SQLite-only code paths never hit: agent_facts NOT NULL vs NULL project-less scope, doc_index concurrent INSERT races without row-level locks on missing rows, and chunks FK requirements absent from the ORM-backed SQLite path.

failureThemes.title: agent_facts project_id NOT NULL violation
failureThemes.rootCause: Schema/code mismatch after unified PostgreSQL migration. the_fork_schema.sql defines agent_facts.project_id as TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '', but agent_memory._project_id_to_db() maps project-less scope ('' / None) to SQL NULL and the ORM model marks project_id nullable=True. SQLite tests create tables via ORM (nullable column) and pass; Alembic on Postgres applies NOT NULL and rejects inserts from set_agent_fact() without project_id.
evidence: CI log: null value in column project_id of relation agent_facts (scout, home_city, Dubai)
the_fork_schema.sql line 130: project_id TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''
agent_memory.py _project_id_to_db maps '' -> None; set_agent_fact inserts project_id=None
failureThemes.recommendedFix: Align project-less scope storage: either persist '' (matching schema DEFAULT) in agent_memory and ORM, or change the_fork_schema.sql to allow NULL project_id and update the unique index semantics. Prefer '' to match existing UNIQUE(agent_name, project_id, key) behavior on Postgres.

failureThemes.title: doc_index concurrent INSERT unique violation
failureThemes.rootCause: PostgreSQL _update_index path uses SELECT FOR UPDATE, which does not lock when the doc_index row is absent. Concurrent writers (cross-process test with spawn) both observe row=None and attempt INSERT, causing duplicate key on doc_index_pkey. SQLite path uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE which serializes writers; Postgres path lacks equivalent mutual exclusion for first-row creation.
evidence: CI log: duplicate key value violates unique constraint doc_index_pkey for project_id=p-xproc
doc_index.py _apply_index_mutation: with_for_update on missing row, then session.add on row is None
failureThemes.recommendedFix: Use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (project_id) DO UPDATE for Postgres upserts, or acquire pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(project_id)) before read-modify-write when the row may not exist.

failureThemes.title: chunks FK violation on bare test project/doc ids
failureThemes.rootCause: PostgreSQL schema enforces chunks.project_id -> projects.id and chunks.doc_id -> documents.id FKs (the_fork_schema.sql). RagChunk ORM intentionally omits FKs for SQLite test convenience. vector_store.upsert_chunks inserts rows without ensuring parent project/document rows exist—works on SQLite, fails on Postgres.
evidence: CI log: chunks_project_id_fkey for proj_x, proj_doc, proj_chat, proj_route
models.py RagChunk comment: ORM omits FK constraints so SQLite test DBs accept arbitrary ids
doc_index.py has _ensure_project_row helper but vector_store.py does not
affectedTests: tests/test_rag.py (index_chunks / retrieve / chat RAG / route tests)
failureThemes.recommendedFix: On PostgreSQL writes, ensure stub Project and Document parent rows exist before upsert_chunks (mirror doc_index._ensure_project_row), or add a shared db helper used by vector_store and doc_index.

failureThemes.title: role root does not exist (healthcheck)
failureThemes.rootCause: Incidental Postgres service health-check noise in CI logs; not a test assertion failure and unrelated to application schema.
evidence: CI log excerpt marks as FATAL healthcheck noise, likely unrelated
failureThemes.recommendedFix: No action required for application code.

notes: Full Postgres pytest run reproduces 37 failures locally; primary themes above account for the CI-logged IntegrityErrors. Remaining failures (hydration KeyError, conversation_history_api, call_llm_daily_cap) likely stem from shared Postgres state / missing test isolation under PYTEST_USE_POSTGRES and warrant separate follow-up.
